file_selection.index_behavior: Directory requests map to index_file only when dir_to_file is true and index_file is configured.
file_selection.mime_derivation: Derive Content-Type from mimetypes.guess_type(candidate); fall back to application/octet-stream.
file_selection.missing_index: Directory requests without a resolvable index return 404 rather than redirecting or listing.
file_selection.precompressed_sidecars: When enabled and accepted, prefer .br or .gz sidecars for whole-response GET/HEAD paths; Range requests stay on the identity representation.
file_selection.validator_generation: Generate strong ETag values from the selected representation bytes and pair them with Last-Modified.

http_semantics.conditional_statuses: Conditional evaluation may produce 304 Not Modified or 412 Precondition Failed before range processing.
http_semantics.content_coding_interaction: Dynamic content coding is bypassed whenever Range is present so byte positions remain deterministic.
http_semantics.head_parity: HEAD preserves the would-be selected representation headers, validators, Content-Length, and range/conditional status while suppressing the body.
http_semantics.if_range: If-Range accepts a matching strong ETag or sufficiently fresh Last-Modified value; otherwise the full representation is served.
http_semantics.range_statuses: Range evaluation may produce 206 Partial Content, 200 full-response fallback, or 416 Range Not Satisfiable with Content-Range: bytes */length.

path_resolution.decode_order: Percent-decode the request path once before mount-relative normalization.
path_resolution.dot_segments: Reject any parent-reference ".." segment after decoding; ignore "." segments and repeated slashes.
path_resolution.hidden_file_policy: Hidden files and directories are treated as ordinary mount-relative names when they remain inside the mount root.
path_resolution.mount_root: Resolve against the configured mount root and require the final candidate to remain under that root after symlink resolution.
path_resolution.separator_policy: Treat "/" as the only valid request-path separator and reject segments containing "\" to keep behavior platform-neutral.
path_resolution.slash_redirects: Do not synthesize slash redirects; a directory resolves to index content only when dir_to_file is enabled and the index file exists.
path_resolution.symlink_policy: Allow symlinks only when the fully resolved target stays within the mount root; deny escaping symlinks.

path_resolution_cases.expected: repeated slashes collapse during PurePosixPath normalization
backslash-containing segment rejected to avoid platform-specific traversal

pathsend.disconnect_race: Client disconnects end the in-flight transfer on a best-effort basis; the package does not retry, rewind, or re-dispatch the file.
pathsend.dispatch_requirements: http.response.pathsend requires an absolute path to an existing regular file.
pathsend.growth_race: Bytes appended after dispatch are not sent once the snapshot length has been fixed.
pathsend.length_snapshot: The server snapshots the file length when it accepts the pathsend event and uses that byte count for transfer planning.
pathsend.shrink_race: If the file shrinks or disappears after dispatch, transfer may terminate early or abort because the response has already started.
pathsend.zero_copy: HTTP/1.1 may use best-effort sendfile when available; HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 stream the planned file segments.
